text: Databases help us store and organize information. Two main types are SQL and NoSQL databases. SQL databases use tables with rows and columns, like a spreadsheet. Each table represents a data entity, such as customers or products. To get specific information, you write queries using structured query language. This makes it easy to find or update data. SQL databases are great when data relationships are important. NoSQL databases are more flexible, storing data in documents or key-value pairs. They work well for large amounts of changing data. Data modeling helps you plan how to structure your database. Indexes speed up searches by creating shortcuts to data.
